items.title: Man faces court charged with murdering elderly mother in her Canberra home

children.content: Marcus Kauffman made a brief appearance in the ACT Magistrates Court this morning, where no bail application was made.

gallery.altText: A crime scene at a residential home in O'Connor.

thumbnail.altText: A crime scene at a residential home in O'Connor.

items.title: Why four lanes might not be better than two on this Canberra road

children.content: The latest federal budget has committed $50 million to duplicate a road in West Belconnen, but transport planning experts warn it could encourage even more peak-hour traffic.

image.altText: Cars travel along a main road past traffic lights. A large hill with a tower at the top is in the background.

items.title: 'Foldable' pistols, 3D-printed guns seized in raid 450m from police station

children.content: A police investigation has uncovered a large cache of weapons in the national capital's suburbs, less than a kilometre from the Belconnen Police Station.

image.altText: A long line of yellow, purple and white 3D-printed firearms.

items.title: CIT chief told she could be fired for disclosing misconduct probe

children.content: Margot McNeill did not tell the Canberra Institute of Technology board she was under investigation for misconduct when she was being recruited.

gallery.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

thumbnail.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

items.title: Long-awaited RSPCA facility given funding boost in federal budget

children.content: Other funding boosts for the ACT revealed in the budget papers include $50 million to duplicate Drake Brockman Drive in West Belconnen, aimed at servicing the area's growing population, and almost $10 million for the National Film and Sound Archive.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses laughs at the camera while holding a dog

items.title: Taser-death inquest told officers had 'plenty of time' to deal with Cooma 95yo

children.content: A senior NSW Ambulance officer tells the Clare Nowland inquest that emergency services could have taken more time responding to the 95-year-old's dementia-related aggression.

image.altText: an elderly woman looking at the camera

items.title: Technicolour frogs released into the wild near Canberra to bolster numbers

children.content: More than 800 tiny, technicoloured northern corroboree frogs are released into Brindabella National Park to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

gallery.altText: An up close of the frogs, with black and yellow markings, in Del Leong's palm.
An up close of the Northern Corroboree frog, with black and yellow markings, in an enclosure.
A tiny black and yellow frog released into the wild, grass.

thumbnail.altText: An up close of the frogs, with black and yellow markings, in Del Leong's palm.
An up close of the Northern Corroboree frog, with black and yellow markings, in an enclosure.
A tiny black and yellow frog released into the wild, grass.

items.title: ABC boss Hugh Marks flags TV cuts as broadcaster pivots to new platforms

children.content: The ABC's managing director says the public broadcaster's biggest challenge is "stopping things" as he seeks to grow audiences on new platforms without increasing staff.

image.altText: Hugh is slightly smiling, wears light shirt, open collar, black  jacket, grey, receding hair, cropped short, anglo.

items.title: Xi made a historical reference to give Trump a warning. What did it mean?

children.content: The US and China have been rivals for almost 80 years, but when the countries' leaders met in Beijing on Thursday, Xi Jinping made reference to a far more ancient feud.

image.altText: Trump and Xi walk past a group of people standing on steps.

items.title: 'We want the straits open': Trump says China agrees on ending the Iran war

children.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping praised the building of a "stable relationship" with the US as the two world leaders met for their second day of talks. Follow live.

image.altText: Trump and Xi, both wearing suits, look at something out of frame to the left

items.title: Cocaine hidden in imported fruit pulp worth estimated $4.6m, AFP says

children.content: Police say criminal syndicates are becoming more creative at concealing drugs within airport shipments.

image.altText: Op Lonsdale 03

items.title: Could Delta win Eurovision? The odds have soared in her favour

children.content: In a dazzling show of light and a powerful, pitch-perfect performance, Delta Goodrem may have just given Australia a real shot at winning Eurovision for the first time.

image.altText: Delta Goodrem singing.

articles.title: Delta Goodrem climbs onto her piano and into Eurovision 2026 grand final

children.content: Delta Goodrem will perform in the Eurovision 2026 grand final for Australia after a rousing performance of the song Eclipse during the semifinal.

image.altText: Delta Goodrem standing on a golden piano with jets of fire behind her.

gallery.altText: Delta Goodrem with her arms outstretched standing behind a golden piano.

thumbnail.altText: Delta Goodrem with her arms outstretched standing behind a golden piano.

items.title: Israeli nationalists chant anti-Arab slogans in Jerusalem Day march

children.content: Thousands of Israeli nationalists, including young boys, join crowds chanting death threats towards Palestinians while marching through Jerusalem Old City.

image.altText: A large crowd of people with many of them holding up Israeli flags.

items.title: Soon after John quit the Liberal Party, the strange phone calls started

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: How Palantir Helps Agencies 'Connect the Dots'

children.content: Palantir doesn’t "spy"—it connects siloed data so agencies can spot patterns fast.
From the CIA/FBI to major companies, the power is in the unified view.
The big question: can we trust it to be used responsibly?

image.altText: A man with gray hair, glasses, and beard wears black shirt and sits in front of a microphone against urple-lit background.

gallery.altText: A man with gray hair, glasses, and beard wears black shirt and sits in front of a microphone against urple-lit background.

thumbnail.altText: A man with gray hair, glasses, and beard wears black shirt and sits in front of a microphone against urple-lit background.

items.title: 'Gut feeling': Rising Socceroos star on why he moved to Norwich City

children.content: Rising Socceroos striker Mo Toure says a "gut feeling" was the reason behind his wildly successful move to the English Championship.

image.altText: Mohamed Toure of Norwich City celebrates after scoring a goal for norwich city

items.title: Sick of maintaining your lawn? Transform it into a native garden

children.content: Many of us are looking to create a native garden as a lower maintenance, more sustainable and often cheaper alternative.

image.altText: close up of lush garden including Australian native plants

items.title: As the eldest daughter, I always knew I would care for my parents

children.content: As the eldest daughter in a Vietnamese family, it was an unspoken expectation I would be the one to care for my aging parents.

gallery.altText: Cindy Mai and her mum when she was 11  years old

thumbnail.altText: Cindy Mai and her mum when she was 11  years old

items.title: Melanie Lynskey joins the fight for justice after NZ's Pike River Mine disaster

children.content: Standing side by side with real-life heroes Anna Osborne and Sonya Rockhouse, the Yellow Jackets star is a passionate ally for their cause.

image.altText: A group of miners in work gear, helmets and head lamps walking.

items.title: National commissioner says review into Kumanjayi Little Baby won't 'cut it'

children.content: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

image.altText: Portrait of Yoorrook Commissioner Sue-Anne Hunter

items.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say

children.content: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.

image.altText: A grandmother holds up her walker and pushes it through a fence while a security guards tries to block her path

articles.title: Activist, 78, arrested at SA golf course, as protesters chain themselves to machinery

children.content: An elderly activist has been arrested after breaking through the gates of the North Adelaide Golf Course, while protesters earlier this morning claimed they had chained themselves to machinery in condemnation of hundreds of trees being cut.

image.altText: A grandmother holds up her walker and pushes it through a fence while a security guards tries to block her path

gallery.altText: A protester sits by a gate of the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

thumbnail.altText: A protester sits by a gate of the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

avatar.alt: A woman with dark blonde hair and a blue jacket looks to the side with serious expression.

items.title: Ruling upheld after transgender woman excluded from female-only app

children.content: ​A landmark Federal Court ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app constituted unlawful gender identity discrimination is upheld on appeal.

image.altText: Sall Grover wears sunglasses and wears smart clothes while walking with two other women.

items.title: Oil trades higher as Trump, Xi discuss war in Iran

children.content: The ASX opened higher in morning trading after rallies on Wall Street, but was slightly down after midday. Oil is trading higher as US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping discuss the war. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The silhouette of an oil pumpjack in the foreground with a wind turbine in the background.

items.title: US vote to halt Iran war narrowly defeated as Israel and Lebanon have 'productive' first day of talks

children.content: Israel and Lebanon have reported a productive first day of talks in Washington as the deadline for the ceasefire between the two countries approaches.

image.altText: Emergency responders gather around a burning vehicle at the scene of an Israeli airstrike

items.title: Matthew Wale becomes Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot win

children.content: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.

image.altText: Matthew

items.title: Surfers conquer 'terrifying' tidal rapid in remote WA

children.content: Pro surfers Anthony Walsh and Dylan Graves have conquered the never-before-surfed Talbot Bay tidal rapid in Western Australia's remote north.

thumbnail.altText: A jetski on a big wave

items.title: Hospitalisation of young babies almost halved by new immunisation program

children.content: A new immunisation program for pregnant mothers and newborns has almost halved the number of young babies being hospitalised with a serious respiratory virus, according to a new report.

image.altText: A young mother with long blonde hair holds up a smiling baby boy

items.title: Man with swastika tattooed on leg arrested in Alice Springs

children.content: A 25-year-old man has been arrested and charged in Alice Springs for displaying a tattoo of a swastika on his leg.

image.altText: A patch of the NT police force emblem on an unidentified officer's uniform

items.title: Overdose at Crown Melbourne every two weeks, data shows

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

image.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

items.title: Clean-up deadline rejected for contaminated abandoned base

children.content: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

image.altText: Images of an abandoned station in the icy and snowy Antarctic wilderness.

items.title: Twenty years since greatest live TV blunder when BBC interviewed wrong guy

children.content: Guy Gomo reflects on his infamous live TV mix-up, which has been immortalised as one of the greatest TV blunders of all time.

items.title: Trust tax could hurt young, not just ultra-wealthy, tax advisors warn

children.content: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.

image.altText: Seveal bank notes laying on dirt.

items.title: Cattle industry loses court appeal over live export ban compensation

children.content: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

image.altText: the sun sets over cattle cattle yard with dust rising

items.title: ABC news quiz: A Eurovision boycott, a pungent frog and a mysterious red globule

children.content: This is not the week to boycott the news quiz, when a particularly ripe frog and a big, red
something
await you.
